concerns.text: The plan cites 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/MMVP/MMVP_VLM/resolve/main/Questions.csv' as the source for 'VLM Reference Scores' to be used as the target variable for cinematic quality dimensions. This dataset (MMVP) is a benchmark for VLM hallucination on visual questions, not a repository of expert-calibrated cinematic quality scores (e.g., 'camera smoothness', 'lighting consistency'). Using this dataset as the ground truth for the main distillation study (FR-003, FR-004) is a fundamental mismatch: the labels do not exist in the cited source, making the core research question unanswerable with the proposed data.

The plan lists 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/xgboost-lover/Skin_Disease_Dataset/...' and '...imagenet-1k-rand_hog/...' as 'Verified' resources for 'CPU-tractability Benchmarks' and 'HOG Features' testing. While these URLs may be reachable, they are inappropriate for the stated purpose. A skin disease dataset cannot validate video feature extraction (optical flow, audio) or video-specific memory profiling. The plan must cite a video-specific benchmark or a synthetic video generator for unit testing the profiling logic, not static image datasets.
